title: carya glabra subsp. megacarpa
intro: Carya glabra subsp. megacarpa, also known as the bigleaf black walnut, is a subspecies of the black walnut tree native to the western United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Carya glabra subsp. megacarpa include its potential to aid in the treatment of various conditions, such as arthritis and skin infections, due to its active compounds like juglone and nuciferin. Carya glabra subsp. megacarpa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tinctures, capsules, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
